{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.5", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {

    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 1,
    "RX.4": 2,
    "RX.5": 4
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Identifies the load-bearing concept (the contribution of the section) and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.",
    "R1.5": "Proposes a coherent end-to-end section plan with per-section rationale.",
    "RX.1": "Flags uncertainty for one claim (the empirical results) but does not mark confidence and missing evidence for all recommendations.",
    "RX.3": "Output covers substantially different active set than requested, focusing on the structure of the section rather than the content.",
    "RX.4": "No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (the overclaims) but does not propose reframing to avoid them.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.8,
    "R1.5": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 0.2,
    "RX.4": 0.6,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: Identifies the load-bearing concept (the contribution of the section) and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.R1.5: Proposes a coherent end-to-end section plan with per-section rationale.
rationale.RX.1: Flags uncertainty for one claim (the empirical results) but does not mark confidence and missing evidence for all recommendations.
rationale.RX.3: Output covers substantially different active set than requested, focusing on the structure of the section rather than the content.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (the overclaims) but does not propose reframing to avoid them.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason.
